# html2md

> html2md is a high-fidelity HTML-to-Markdown converter and web scraper. It uses headless Chrome with advanced stealth plugins to bypass WAFs and Cloudflare, renders JavaScript-heavy SPAs, and extracts clean Markdown using Readability.js and Turndown.js. Designed for AI agents, RAG pipelines, and data extraction workflows.

- REST API with three response modes: `format=json` (structured JSON, default for tools), `format=markdown` (raw text), `format=stream` (NDJSON logs + `__JSON__` result, default for Web UI)
- Four conversion methods: `method=auto` (default, tries native→static→browser), `native`, `static` (cheerio/Readability), `browser` (headless Puppeteer)
- Zero-friction browser-bar conversion: `GET /https://example.com` returns Markdown instantly
- Rate-limited by tier: Free (50 req/15min), Pro (500/15min), Enterprise (5000/15min) — pass `Authorization: Bearer <key>`
- Long-running endpoints support `async: true` for immediate `202 Accepted` + optional `webhook_url` callback with HMAC-SHA256 signature
- Cache control: append `?maxAge=3600` (serve cache only if fresher than N seconds) or `?force=true` (bypass cache)
- Token counting headers: `x-markdown-tokens`, `x-html-tokens`, `x-word-count`
- If a client disconnects mid-stream, the server kills the headless browser processes to conserve memory

---

## REST API Reference

Base URL: `{{BASE_URL}}` (default port `8222`, configurable via `PORT` env var)

Start the server:

```bash
node server.js
```

### Response Formats

All core endpoints support the `format` parameter:

| Value      | Behavior                                                             |
| ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `json`     | Returns a clean JSON object (default for programmatic access)        |
| `markdown` | Returns raw Markdown text (`/api/convert` only)                      |
| `stream`   | Streams NDJSON log events, appends `__JSON__` result (Web UI default)|

### Conversion Methods

Control the extraction engine with the `method` parameter:

| Value    | Behavior                                                                 |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `auto`   | Default. Tries `native` → `static` → `browser` in sequence              |
| `native` | Fetches raw Markdown if the target server supports content negotiation   |
| `static` | Lightweight cheerio/Readability extraction without executing JavaScript  |
| `browser`| Full headless Puppeteer rendering for JS-heavy SPAs                      |

### Rate Limits

| Tier       | Limit          | Auth                          |
| ---------- | -------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Free       | 50 req / 15min | No key (IP-based)             |
| Pro        | 500 / 15min    | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` |
| Enterprise | 5000 / 15min   | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` |

Rate limit headers (`RateLimit-Limit`, `RateLimit-Remaining`, `RateLimit-Reset`) are included in every response.

### Async Workflows

Long-running endpoints (`/api/batch`, `/api/crawl`, `/api/agentify`) support async execution:

- Pass `"async": true` in the request body → immediate `202 Accepted` with `job_id`
- Optionally pass `"webhook_url": "https://..."` → HTTP POST callback on completion
- Optionally pass `"email": "you@example.com"` → branded email from `noreply@traylinx.com` with a secure, token-signed download link (valid 72 hours)
- Webhook payloads are signed with `X-Webhook-Signature` (HMAC-SHA256) if `WEBHOOK_SECRET` is configured
- Poll job status via `GET /api/jobs/:id`
- Job results include `emailStatus` (`sent`, `failed`, or `null`) and `emailError` fields

---

### Health Check

```
GET /api/health
```

Response:

```json
{ "status": "ok", "api": "html2md", "version": "2.0.0", "time": "2026-03-04T12:00:00.000Z" }
```

### Zero-Friction URL-Prepend

```
GET /https://example.com
```

Prepend the API domain to any URL. Returns clean Markdown by default. Add `?format=json` for JSON.

### Upload Info

```
GET /api/upload-info
```

Returns supported file extensions, families, browser accept string, and max file size (100 MB).

### Convert Single URL

```
POST /api/convert
Content-Type: application/json
```

Request body:

```json
{
  "url": "https://example.com/article",
  "downloadImages": true,
  "frontMatter": true,
  "waitMs": 10000,
  "maxImageSizeMb": 10,
  "format": "json",
  "method": "auto"
}
```

| Field            | Type    | Default  | Description                     |
| ---------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------- |
| `url`            | string  | —        | URL to convert (required)       |
| `downloadImages` | boolean | `true`   | Download referenced images      |
| `frontMatter`    | boolean | `true`   | Add YAML front matter           |
| `waitMs`         | number  | `10000`  | JS rendering timeout (ms)       |
| `maxImageSizeMb` | number  | `10`     | Max image size to download      |
| `format`         | string  | `json`   | `json`, `markdown`, or `stream` |
| `method`         | string  | `auto`   | `auto`, `native`, `static`, `browser` |

Response (`format=json`):

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "url": "https://example.com/article",
  "markdown": "# Article Title\n\nContent here...",
  "method": "static",
  "metadata": { "title": "Article Title", "author": null },
  "tokens": { "html": 18450, "md": 3125 },
  "quality": { "wordCount": 1150, "headings": 12, "links": 24 }
}
```

### Batch Convert

```
POST /api/batch
Content-Type: application/json
```

Request body:

```json
{
  "urls": ["https://example.com/page1", "https://example.com/page2"],
  "downloadImages": true,
  "frontMatter": true,
  "format": "json",
  "async": true,
  "webhook_url": "https://your-server.com/hook"
}
```

| Field            | Type     | Default | Description                              |
| ---------------- | -------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `urls`           | string[] | —       | Array of URLs (required)                 |
| `downloadImages` | boolean  | `true`  | Download images                          |
| `frontMatter`    | boolean  | `true`  | Add YAML front matter                    |
| `screenshot`     | boolean  | `false` | Capture screenshots per page             |
| `method`         | string   | `auto`  | `auto`, `native`, `static`, `browser`    |
| `format`         | string   | `json`  | `json` or `stream`                       |
| `preset`         | string   | `full`  | `full`, `compact`, or `chunks`           |
| `async`          | boolean  | `false` | Return 202 immediately, process in background |
| `webhook_url`    | string   | —       | URL to receive POST callback on completion    |
| `email`          | string   | —       | Email to notify on completion with download link (72h expiry) |

Sync response (`format=json`):

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "results": [
    { "url": "https://example.com/page1", "success": true, "markdown": "# Page 1..." },
    { "url": "https://example.com/page2", "success": true, "markdown": "# Page 2..." }
  ]
}
```

Async response (`async: true`):

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "job_id": "j_abc123",
  "status": "running",
  "status_url": "/api/jobs/j_abc123",
  "result_url": "/api/jobs/j_abc123/result"
}
```

### Crawl Site

```
POST /api/crawl
Content-Type: application/json
```

The crawler automatically checks `<target>/llms.txt` before spawning Chromium. If found, seed URLs from `llms.txt` are added to the BFS queue for guided discovery.

Request body:

```json
{
  "url": "https://docs.example.com",
  "depth": 3,
  "maxPages": 50,
  "treeOnly": true,
  "async": true,
  "webhook_url": "https://your-server.com/hook"
}
```

| Field         | Type    | Default | Description                              |
| ------------- | ------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `url`         | string  | —       | Root URL to crawl (required)             |
| `depth`       | number  | `3`     | Max crawl depth                          |
| `maxPages`    | number  | `50`    | Max pages to discover                    |
| `treeOnly`    | boolean | `false` | Return site tree without converting      |
| `async`       | boolean | `false` | Return 202 immediately                   |
| `webhook_url` | string  | —       | URL to receive callback on completion    |
| `email`       | string  | —       | Email to notify on completion with download link (72h expiry) |

Response with `treeOnly: true`:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "tree": "docs.example.com/\n├── getting-started/\n├── api-reference/\n└── changelog/",
  "urls": [
    "https://docs.example.com",
    "https://docs.example.com/getting-started",
    "https://docs.example.com/api-reference"
  ],
  "stats": { "depth": 3, "maxPages": 50 },
  "llmsGuided": true,
  "llmsUrlCount": 5
}
```

### Agentify

```
POST /api/agentify
Content-Type: application/json
```

Generate an AI-ready Skill Bundle from a website.

```json
{
  "url": "https://example.com/docs",
  "maxPages": 20,
  "apiKey": "sk-lf-your-switchai-key",
  "targetAgent": "web",
  "async": true,
  "webhook_url": "https://your-server.com/hook"
}
```

| Field              | Type     | Default | Description                              |
| ------------------ | -------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `url`              | string   | —       | Target URL (required)                    |
| `maxPages`         | number   | `50`    | Max pages to crawl                       |
| `apiKey`           | string   | —       | SwitchAI API key (enables AI synthesis)  |
| `targetAgent`      | string   | `web`   | `web` or `local`                         |
| `includeApiSchema` | boolean  | `false` | Generate API schemas                     |
| `urls`             | string[] | `[]`    | Optional predefined URL list             |
| `async`            | boolean  | `false` | Return 202 immediately                   |
| `webhook_url`      | string   | —       | Callback on completion                   |

### File2MD

```
POST /api/file2md
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
```

Upload or URL-fetch files for OCR/transcription to Markdown.

| Field    | Type   | Default | Description                              |
| -------- | ------ | ------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `file`   | File   | —       | File to convert (multipart upload)       |
| `url`    | string | —       | Or: direct URL to a file                 |
| `apiKey` | string | —       | SwitchAI API key (required)              |
| `enhance`| string | `true`  | AI-polish the raw OCR output             |
| `model`  | string | —       | Override the vision/LLM model            |
| `format` | string | `json`  | `json` or `stream`                       |

Either `file` or `url` must be provided.

### Download Archive

```
GET /api/download/:site
```

Downloads all crawled content for the given domain as a ZIP archive.

### Job Management

```
GET  /api/jobs              — List all jobs (filter by x-client-id header)
GET  /api/jobs/:id          — Get job metadata (includes emailStatus, emailError)
GET  /api/jobs/:id/result   — Fetch completed job result
GET  /api/jobs/:id/logs     — Real-time SSE log stream for running jobs
DELETE /api/jobs            — Clear registry (optional ?before=ISO-Date)
```

### Token-Signed Downloads

```
GET /api/download/job/:id?token=...
```

Secure, time-limited download endpoint for async job results. The token is an HMAC-SHA256 signed URL generated when the job completes. Links expire after 72 hours (`JOB_TTL_HOURS`). This URL is included in email notifications and webhook payloads.

### Cache Management

```
GET    /api/cache/stats         — Cache statistics (total pages, per-site counts)
DELETE /api/cache/:hostname     — Purge all cached pages for a domain
```

---

## CLI Reference

```
bin/html2md [options] <url|file>
bin/html2md --batch <urls.txt>
bin/html2md --crawl <url> [--depth N]
bin/html2md --crawl <url> --tree-only
```

| Flag                    | Description                        | Default                   |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| `-o, --output DIR`      | Custom output directory            | Auto-generated in `jobs/` |
| `-d, --download-images` | Download referenced images         | `true`                    |
| `--no-images`           | Skip image downloading             | -                         |
| `--front-matter`        | Add YAML front matter to output    | `false`                   |
| `-w, --wait MS`         | Puppeteer JS rendering wait time   | `10000`                   |
| `--max-image-size MB`   | Skip images larger than N MB       | `10`                      |
| `--concurrency N`       | Parallel jobs for batch/crawl      | `3`                       |
| `--depth N`             | Max crawl depth                    | `3`                       |
| `--max-pages N`         | Max pages to crawl                 | `300`                     |
| `--tree-only`           | Show site tree but skip conversion | `false`                   |
| `-q, --quiet`           | Suppress progress output           | `false`                   |
| `-v, --verbose`         | Show detailed script output        | `false`                   |

Examples:

```bash
# Convert single URL
./bin/html2md https://example.com

# Convert with YAML front matter
./bin/html2md https://blog.example.com/post --front-matter

# Batch convert from file
./bin/html2md --batch urls.txt --concurrency 5

# Crawl a docs site
./bin/html2md --crawl https://docs.example.com --depth 2 --max-pages 50

# Preview site tree without converting
./bin/html2md --crawl https://example.com --tree-only
```

---

## Architecture

Every URL goes through a multi-step pipeline with automatic method selection:

```
URL → Method Selection → Fetch → Extract → Convert → Download → Polish → page.md
```

### Method Selection (Phase 1)

The `auto` method tries extraction in order of efficiency:
1. **Native** — HTTP content negotiation (`Accept: text/markdown`)
2. **Static** — cheerio + Readability.js (no browser, fast)
3. **Browser** — Full Puppeteer rendering (JS-heavy SPAs)

### Fetch (`scripts/fetch.js`)

Renders the page using Puppeteer (headless Chrome) with stealth evasion:
- `puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth` for Cloudflare/WAF bypass
- Rotating realistic Desktop User-Agents
- Browser `Sec-Fetch-*` and `Accept-Language` headers
- `navigator.webdriver` masking

### Extract (`scripts/extract.js`)

Extracts main content using a three-tier cascade:
1. **Readability.js** (primary) — Mozilla's algorithm
2. **Trafilatura** (Python fallback) — for difficult pages
3. **Manual strip** (last resort) — removes `<script>`, `<style>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>`

### Convert (`scripts/convert.js`)

Converts clean HTML to Markdown using Turndown.js with GFM plugin.

### Download (`scripts/download.js`)

Downloads all referenced images, rewrites paths, handles base64 data URIs.

### Polish (`scripts/polish.js`)

Final normalization: fixes blank lines, normalizes headings, adds YAML front matter, calculates quality metrics.

---

## Agentic Skills

- [html2md-scraper](/skills/html2md-scraper.md): Actionable instructions, usage guidelines, and CLI architectures for autonomous software agents.

## Getting Started

Prerequisites: Node.js 18+, Python 3 (optional for Trafilatura fallback).

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Convert a single URL
./bin/html2md https://example.com

# Start the API server
node server.js
# Server runs on http://localhost:8222

# Use the API (format=json default)
curl -X POST http://localhost:8222/api/convert \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://example.com"}'
```

Job output folder structure:

```
jobs/<job-name>/
├── input/
│   ├── raw.html            # Original HTML
│   ├── rendered.html       # Rendered DOM after JavaScript
│   └── screenshot.png      # Page screenshot
├── processing/
│   ├── extracted.html      # Clean content after extraction
│   └── log.txt             # Processing log
├── output/
│   ├── page.md             # Final Markdown output
│   └── assets/             # Downloaded images
└── job.json                # Job metadata and quality metrics
```
